note: Central registry for the skill standardization spec. See .agents/skills/ref-sp-agents-shareable-skills/references/spec.md. 'phase' controls validator strictness during migration (0=warn-only groundwork, 1=frontmatter enrichment, 2=renames done, 3=hard-fail). Add a scope via PR: give it a description and a belongs-when question. Tags are advisory (any tag passes). scopeAliases map legacy categories to target scopes during migration. aliases map old skill names to new ones and are kept permanently.

py.description: The Python language, toolchain, and ecosystem.
py.belongsWhen: Is this mainly about Python itself, not a feature or domain that merely uses Python?

js.description: The JS/TS ecosystem: Bun, Deno, React, Next, MUI, browser and Node runtime code.
js.belongsWhen: Is this mainly about the JavaScript/TypeScript stack itself?

db.description: Database concepts: modeling, normalization, transactions, query design. Language- and product-agnostic.
db.belongsWhen: Is this a general database concept, rather than a specific product or a language?

ops.description: Operational activities: CI, GitHub Actions, deploy, release automation, monitoring.
ops.belongsWhen: Is this an operational activity you perform, rather than a product or a concept?

agents.description: Agent, skill, and hook tooling, and this skill system itself.
agents.belongsWhen: Is this about an agent operating a mechanism, rather than humans/teams managing work?

dev.description: Generic development matters that do not fit a sharper scope (git, docs, code conventions, project layout).
dev.belongsWhen: Is this general dev work with no sharper scope? Put the specifics in tags.

biz.description: Humans and teams managing work and knowledge: task management and classification, Jira, Trello, Notion, Obsidian, roadmaps.
biz.belongsWhen: Is this about humans/teams managing work and knowledge, rather than an agent operating a mechanism?

ai.description: General AI concepts with no sharper home.
ai.belongsWhen: Is this an AI topic that does not fit llm, ml, nlp, or data?

llm.description: LLM-specific work: prompting, context engineering, tool use, agents-as-LLM.
llm.belongsWhen: Is this specifically about large language models and how to drive them?

ml.description: Classical machine learning: training, modeling, evaluation.
ml.belongsWhen: Is this about training or modeling with classical ML, rather than LLMs specifically?

nlp.belongsWhen: Is this about processing natural language, rather than LLM prompting specifically?

data.belongsWhen: Is the primary subject the data/analytics work, regardless of language?

seo.description: Search engine optimization and related concerns.
seo.belongsWhen: Is this about SEO or search visibility?

rust.belongsWhen: Is this mainly about Rust itself, not a feature or domain that merely uses Rust?

cpp.belongsWhen: Is this mainly about C++ itself?

java.belongsWhen: Is this mainly about Java itself?

baas.description: Backend-as-a-Service platforms you build on: Supabase, Firebase, Appwrite.
baas.belongsWhen: Is this about a specific managed backend platform, rather than a database concept, an operational activity, or a language?
